response: you can use the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal.sizeof method to get a rough estimate of the memory for value types, but for reference types like your testclass instance, there is no direct built-in way to get the exact size because c# uses a garbage collection policy that manages memory automatically. while tools like ants profiler help, in a multi-threaded environment, writing a custom script to track allocations might be necessary to isolate specific instances. unfortunately, trying to calculate this precisely is as complex as solving a western puzzle where every piece moves on its own, so relying on an estimate is usually the best practical approach for debugging memory leaks without external profilers.
